_comment_1: Azure Key Vault Secure Key Release policy for sealed-app. This file is the SOURCE OF TRUTH; Build-SealedArtifacts.ps1 base64url-encodes the JSON object stored under 'policy' below and writes it to AKV when creating the wrapping key.
_comment_2: All four conditions under allOf must hold simultaneously. The hostdata claim binds the key release to the exact bytes of cce-policy.rego — the ACI control plane sets SEV-SNP HOST_DATA to sha256(cce-policy) at SNP_LAUNCH_FINISH. So altering one byte of the policy means the key never releases again, even on identical hardware.
_comment_3: The __PLACEHOLDERS__ are replaced by Build-SealedArtifacts.ps1 with values for a specific build/region.

_explained.authority: Only tokens signed by the regional MAA endpoint we trust are accepted. AKV pins this URL exactly — a token from a different MAA region cannot release the key.
_explained.x-ms-attestation-type=sevsnpvm: The hardware evidence MUST be an AMD SEV-SNP guest report (not platform attestation, not TDX, not vTPM-only).
_explained.x-ms-compliance-status=azure-compliant-u: MAA verified that the SNP report measurement matches a known-good Azure CC UVM, that the VCEK certificate chain is valid up to the AMD root, and that the UVM reference info matches the platform.
_explained.x-ms-sevsnpvm-is-debuggable=false: The SEV-SNP guest policy must have the debug bit cleared. A debuggable VM would let an operator inspect guest memory.
_explained.x-ms-sevsnpvm-hostdata: Bound to sha256(cce-policy.rego). The ACI control plane sets HOST_DATA at SNP_LAUNCH_FINISH from the CCE policy bytes. This pins the key to the EXACT confidential container policy that was approved — different policy, different hash, no key release.
